app/api/scrape/route.ts: The POST handler is a 180-line function mixing HTTP parsing, state-machine orchestration, Reddit scraping, Google Places enrichment, DB upsert, and community-pick insertion. It has 5 distinct responsibility clusters in a single function body.

components/LoadMoreButton.tsx: 213-line component mixing data-fetching (fetch call), data transformation (groupByName, shouldMerge, normalizeWebsite), client-side state management, and JSX rendering. groupByName/shouldMerge/normalizeWebsite are duplicated verbatim from lib/search.ts.

app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx: mixes DB access (two separate prisma queries), grouping logic (groupRestaurantsByName call), date serialization, and full page rendering. The groupRestaurantsByName call fetches ALL city restaurants just to find the group for one restaurant — a performance design issue embedded in the rendering layer.

components/TrendingSection.tsx: The useEffect callback is 30+ lines mixing geolocation API, two different fetch paths (geo+IP fallback), and local-storage writes. High cyclomatic complexity (>15 branches) is correctly flagged.

lib/google-places.ts: searchGooglePlaces mixes API call, type-filtering, validation, and photo-URL resolution (an additional async network call) in one function. The resolvePhotoUrl call inside the main function creates an unexpected network round-trip not visible from the function signature.

app/api/geo/route.ts: Small file (36 LOC) that is correctly focused — handles coordinate-based and IP-based geo. Flagged as orphaned (Next.js API routes have no static importers) but has a coherent single responsibility. DISMISS orphaned concern.

app/api/vote/route.ts: The POST handler mixes validation, duplicate-check, DB transaction, and state-transition logic. At 96 LOC this is dense but has a coherent single responsibility (process a recommendation vote). The complexity is inherent to the domain.

app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts: Structurally identical to vote/route.ts — same pattern, same shape. The boilerplate duplication signal is valid but the two handlers serve genuinely different entities (restaurant vs recommendation vote).

components/RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx and VoteButton.tsx: The `cast` function is duplicated verbatim (optimistic update → fetch → reconcile/revert). The only differences are the API endpoint and storage key prefix. Extraction is warranted.
prisma/seed.ts: A script, not a module. Used via `npx prisma db seed`. The orphaned signal is a false positive for seed files. The 18x hardcoded URL smell is expected for seed data.
lib/reddit.ts: isValidRestaurantName, extractRestaurantName, and isPostRelevantToQuery are well-focused single-purpose functions. scrapeRedditForRestaurants coordinates them with clear flow.
lib/search.ts: groupRestaurantsByName and shouldMergeGroups are cohesive — the merging logic is complex but belongs together.

lib/geo.ts: `detect_city` is exported as the primary function, but `detectCityFromIp` is also exported as an alias (`export const detectCityFromIp = detect_city`). The alias is used in app/api/geo/route.ts while the canonical name is used in app/api/trending/route.ts and app/api/search/route.ts. The module presents two names for the same function without a clear migration comment — a surface-level API inconsistency.

lib/openrouter.ts: the file-level JSDoc says 'using Gemini Flash' but the actual model used is `meta-llama/llama-3.2-3b-instruct:free` (not Gemini Flash). The comment was written for a different model and was not updated when the model changed — a docstring/signature divergence.

lib/search.ts: `parseQuery` docstring says 'using Gemini Flash' (inherited from openrouter.ts comment style) but the function delegates to `parseQueryWithLLM` which uses a Llama model. Minor doc drift.
lib/validate.ts: all `validateX` functions declare `void` return type and throw on failure — they honor their contracts. No silent swallowing or hidden returns.

lib/rules.ts: `checkRule03` and `checkRule05` return `boolean` — `true` means vote allowed, `false` means rejected. The naming `checkRuleXX` doesn't communicate the boolean polarity. A caller unfamiliar with the codebase might misread `if (!allowed)` as a double-negative. The return value semantics diverge slightly from the function name's implied meaning (check ≠ allow).

lib/state-machine.ts: `assertValidTransition` and `isValidTransition` have complementary contracts that are both honored. The module exports three named functions with clear, distinct contracts — well-designed.
lib/google-places.ts: `searchGooglePlaces` declares `Promise<PlaceDetails[]>` return type. The function always returns an array (possibly empty) — no null path. Contract is honored.

lib/reddit.ts: `scrapeRedditForRestaurants` and `fetchCommunityPicksForRestaurant` both declare `Promise<ExtractedRestaurant[]>` / `Promise<CommunityPick[]>`. Both always return arrays. Contract honored.
lib/openrouter.ts: `parseQueryWithLLM` declares `Promise<{ city: string | null; terms: string }>`. All code paths return this shape (fallback also returns it). Contract honored.
app/api/* route handlers: all handlers declare no explicit return type (Next.js convention) but consistently return `NextResponse.json(...)`. No contract mismatch.

lib/reddit.ts: `foodQuery` function (line 100-102) takes a string and returns the same string unchanged — it is a no-op wrapper. Its name implies transformation but it performs none. This is a hidden contract lie (no-op with a meaningful name).

lib/geo.ts: the JSDoc for `detect_city` says 'Alias: detect_city (used by GeoDetector.detect_city in the spec)' which implies the primary name IS detect_city, yet the alias `detectCityFromIp` is exported for 'backward compatibility'. This reversal (alias documented as canonical, canonical exported as alias) is confusing.

lib/hours.ts: `isOpenNow` returns `boolean | null`. The null case is documented (cannot parse). All code paths return one of the three values. Contract is technically honored but callers must handle null, which is not communicated in the function name (`isOpenNow` implies boolean).

lib/openrouter.ts: The `regexFallback` function (lines 142-151) — the function is declared as a named function and is only called within the same module. The match case correctly returns a split city/terms; the no-match case returns `{ city: null, terms: trimmed }`. Logic is direct and correct.

lib/openrouter.ts 'async functions without await' smell: the `regexFallback` function is NOT async — the smell detector may have flagged something else. Reviewing the actual file: all async functions (parseQueryWithLLM, getRedditRecommendations) do contain await calls. The flagged smell may be a false positive for this file.

lib/search.ts 'async functions without await': `parseQuery` is async and awaits `parseQueryWithLLM`. `searchRestaurants` is async and awaits `prisma.restaurant.findMany`. `countSearchResults` is async and awaits `prisma.restaurant.count`. No async-without-await found in lib/search.ts on manual inspection — likely a false positive from the detector.

app/search/page.tsx 'async functions without await': The `SearchResults` async component (line 19) awaits `parseQuery` (line 21) and `searchRestaurants` (line 33). However, `SearchPage` (line 101) is a non-async function — not flagged. The smell appears to target something within the component. On inspection: no obvious async-without-await defect.

app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx 'async functions without await': `generateMetadata` (line 32) awaits `prisma.restaurant.findUnique`. `RestaurantPage` (line 41) awaits two prisma calls. The smell signal may be misfiring on nested functions or the component itself — no confirmed defect.

lib/reddit.ts: `foodQuery(query: string): string { return query; }` — synchronous function, not async. The no-op body is a logic clarity issue (function claims to transform but doesn't) but not an async/await issue.

lib/hours.ts: `isOpenNow` has complex branching (>15 branches confirmed). The branch structure is inherent to parsing multiple time formats (12h, 24h, compact, weekday ranges). Each branch is distinct and handles a different format — not identical branches.
lib/reddit.ts: `isRecommendationRequest` (lines 162-164) — returns `REQUEST_PATTERNS.some((p) => p.test(title))`. Correct and direct.
lib/reddit.ts: `isValidRestaurantName` (lines 190-202) — sequential guard clauses each returning false or true. Direct and readable.

lib/validate.ts: All validators follow an identical pattern of checking null/undefined then empty string for each field. Each field check is an independent guard clause — no redundant logic. There are no boolean simplifications needed.
lib/search.ts: `shouldMergeGroups` has a return at each branch — direct early-return pattern. No always-true/false conditions found.

components/TrendingSection.tsx: The `isOpenNow` call at line 100 returns `boolean | null`. The ternary `openNow !== null && (...)` is a correct null guard, not a redundant check — the value genuinely can be null.

app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx line 75: `const totalNetVotes = (restaurant.upvotes ?? 0) - (restaurant.downvotes ?? 0)`. After the `if (!restaurant) notFound()` guard, `restaurant` is guaranteed non-null by TypeScript's narrowing. The `?? 0` fallback on `upvotes` and `downvotes` is redundant if the Prisma schema defines these as non-nullable integers. However, since Prisma returns typed non-nullable Int fields, the `?? 0` is defensive but not provably redundant without schema inspection.

components/RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx: `const [upvotes, setUpvotes] = useState(initialUpvotes ?? 0)` — `initialUpvotes` is typed as `number` in the props interface, making `?? 0` redundant. TypeScript guarantees it is never undefined/null at this call site.

lib/search.ts: The `groupRestaurantsByName` function has a while loop with a nested O(n²) scan for merging. The loop is logically correct (terminates when no merges happen) but the termination condition depends on the data. Not a logic error.

app/api/scrape/route.ts: The state machine transitions are asserted sequentially with `assertValidTransition` and immediate `state = '...'`. On line 141-144: after `confirmed.length === 0`, the code calls `assertValidTransition(state, 'FAILED')` then `return` — but `state = 'FAILED'` is set AFTER the return statement conceptually (the variable is set before return on line 143). The transition is correctly asserted before the early return.

lib/validate.ts: all validators accept `Record<string, unknown>` rather than the typed interfaces that exist in the same codebase (ParsedQuery, RedditPost, ExtractedRestaurant, PlaceDetails). Each call site casts with `as unknown as Record<string, unknown>`. The typed interfaces exist but are bypassed at the validation boundary.

app/api/scrape/route.ts line 173: `validateRestaurantInput({...} as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)` — the restaurant data is assembled as a typed object and then immediately cast away from its type for validation. The double-cast (through unknown) is the standard TypeScript escape hatch used to suppress type checking.

lib/reddit.ts lines 131-135: `.filter((r: unknown) => r && typeof (r as Record<string, unknown>).name === 'string' && ...)` — the parsed API response is typed as unknown and then manually narrowed inline. This is correct at the API boundary but could benefit from a typed guard function.

lib/google-places.ts: `buildPlaceDetails(place: Record<string, unknown>, apiKey: string)` — the place parameter accepts any Record shape. Google Places API responses are cast to Record<string, unknown> throughout. Given the external API shape cannot be statically typed without a generated client, this is acceptable at the boundary.

components/LoadMoreButton.tsx: `const newGroups = groupByName(data.results as RawRestaurant[], seenNames)` — the fetch response is cast to `RawRestaurant[]` without runtime shape validation. If the API response shape changes, the cast will silently pass while runtime behavior breaks.

components/TrendingSection.tsx: `TrendingRestaurant` interface is defined locally in the component. The API response is cast via `.then((d) => { setCity(d.city); setResults(d.results ?? []) })` — `d` is typed as `any` (implicit from fetch().json()). The results array is assigned to `TrendingRestaurant[]` state without a runtime check.
lib/search.ts: `RestaurantWithRecommendations` interface correctly mirrors the Prisma return type. The `total_net_votes` field is a computed field added via `.map()` — this pattern is sound.

app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx: `groupRestaurantsByName(cityRestaurants.map((r) => ({ ...r, total_net_votes: r.upvotes - r.downvotes })))` — the spread preserves all Prisma fields but the explicit `total_net_votes` addition is untyped relative to RestaurantWithRecommendations. TypeScript infers the correct type via structural inference here.

lib/hours.ts: `isOpenNow(hours: string): boolean | null` — the return type honestly documents the null case. All three values (true, false, null) are reachable via different code paths. Type is accurate.
lib/fingerprint.ts: `computeFingerprint(request: NextRequest): string` — always returns a hex string. Type is accurate.
lib/state-machine.ts: All types are exact literal union types. `VALID_TRANSITIONS` is typed as `Record<ScrapeState, ScrapeState[]>` which guarantees exhaustive coverage of all states.
lib/openrouter.ts: `parseQueryWithLLM` returns `Promise<{ city: string | null; terms: string }>`. The type is honest — city can be null on all code paths (fallback, API failure, explicit null parse).
lib/reddit.ts: The `cachedToken` module variable is typed as `{ token: string; expiresAt: number } | null` — accurate. The token field is correctly narrowed before use.

components/RestaurantCard.tsx: The `Restaurant` interface has `status: string` rather than the `RestaurantStatus` enum from Prisma. The `STATUS_LABELS` Record uses string keys, so string comparisons are used throughout instead of the enum — an enum bypass pattern.
components/TrendingSection.tsx: Same pattern — `status: string` in `TrendingRestaurant` instead of `RestaurantStatus` enum.

app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx: `STATUS_INFO: Record<string, {...}>` with string keys 'VERIFIED', 'UNREVIEWED', 'INCOMPLETE' — raw string literal comparisons instead of the RestaurantStatus enum from @prisma/client.

app/api/scrape/route.ts: body typed as `{ city?: string; query?: string }` — both fields are optional, which correctly reflects the JSON parsing uncertainty. Narrowing is done with explicit checks before use.

lib/validate.ts: `as unknown as Record<string, unknown>` double-cast is used 8+ times across the codebase. This is a systemic pattern that erases type information at every validation call site. The validators were designed for Record<string, unknown> inputs but are called with fully-typed objects, creating a mismatch between the codebase's type-richness and the validation layer's type-blindness.

cross_module_architecture.dimension_character: The codebase has a clear and respected three-layer boundary: lib/ (domain logic), app/api/ (HTTP handlers), components/ (UI), with dependency direction consistently pointing inward. The primary structural weakness is a restaurant-grouping algorithm that lives in both lib/search.ts and components/LoadMoreButton.tsx — the client and server each maintain their own copy of the same merge logic, creating a genuine missing-boundary problem that will cause divergence over time. The geo alias bifurcation (detect_city vs detectCityFromIp) is a minor naming oddity at a boundary but does not create a cycle or direction violation.
cross_module_architecture.score_rationale: The architecture scores solidly for respected layering and no import cycles, which represents the core contract for cross_module_architecture. The score is held below 90 by the duplicated grouping algorithm — a concrete cross-package duplication that indicates a missing shared boundary. At the 80-range anchor (recurring boundary violations that make changes ripple unexpectedly), this is a single well-defined duplication rather than a systemic pattern, placing it closer to 82 than 75.

high_level_elegance.dimension_character: The top-level decomposition is domain-aligned and a new engineer can trace any user action to its responsible module without confusion. The lib/ layer is cohesive and the API routes follow a predictable pattern. The primary decomposition weakness is that the scrape route acts as both an HTTP handler and a pipeline orchestrator — the upsertRestaurant function, state machine transitions, validation calls, and multi-source coordination all live inside the route handler rather than in a focused lib/ module. A secondary weakness is the status badge concept replicated with minor variations across three files.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The overall structure is solid and a new engineer would have a reliable mental model within an hour. The score sits at 84 rather than 90 because the scrape route's dual responsibility (HTTP + orchestration) is a recurring pattern that a team member would notice immediately and find friction with — it is not a catastrophic ownership failure but it is a predictable change-hazard. The status badge fragmentation is a smaller but similarly predictable friction point.

convention_outlier.dimension_character: The codebase is largely convention-consistent within each directory, but has several well-defined outliers: the snake_case detect_city function in an otherwise uniformly camelCase lib/ directory, a mid-file import in lib/reddit.ts that breaks the universal top-of-file import pattern, and a dead VoteButton.tsx sibling whose replacement (RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx) duplicates its core logic with minor variations. The status badge naming drift (STATUS_LABELS vs STATUS_BADGE for identical shapes) is a smaller friction point. None of these rise to style-island level, but they are concentrated in the most-trafficked files.
convention_outlier.score_rationale: The 74 reflects that a developer learning patterns from one file will encounter one or two surprises in siblings — the naming drift between STATUS_LABELS and STATUS_BADGE, the detect_city vs detectCityFromIp split, and the dead VoteButton competing with its replacement are all concrete points where a developer would say 'why is this different?' The codebase is not fragmented at the 60-level but it has enough visible outliers to sit below 80.

error_consistency.dimension_character: The HTTP boundary (app/api/) has consistent try/catch + JSON error response handling. The domain layer (lib/) has genuinely mixed strategies: some modules throw typed errors, others return null, and several silently swallow errors after logging — making it impossible to predict from the call site whether a failure will propagate or be absorbed. The most consequential inconsistency is in the vote flow, where tryTransitionToVerified is called outside a transaction and its failure would return a 500 even though the underlying vote write already committed.
error_consistency.score_rationale: The 68 reflects that a developer writing against these lib/ functions cannot rely on the error contract — they must read each implementation to know whether a failure will throw, return null, or disappear into a console.warn. The vote/transition sequencing bug is a concrete behavioral problem, not just a style inconsistency. This places the codebase in the 60-70 range: error behavior is not catastrophically opaque, but it is unreliable enough that a developer must read each module to understand failure modes.

naming_quality.dimension_character: Naming quality is strong throughout lib/ — domain terms are precise, prefixes are consistent, and a developer can predict function behavior from the name in almost all cases. The one concrete outlier is foodQuery in lib/reddit.ts, which is named as a transformation but is actually an identity function. The get/build/parse/validate/is/check prefix taxonomy is well-established and used uniformly.
naming_quality.score_rationale: The 83 reflects a codebase where naming is reliable and a developer would rarely need to open an implementation just to understand what a function does. The score is held from 90 by the foodQuery no-op name (a developer would open it expecting to see query preprocessing and find nothing), and the minor detect_city/detectCityFromIp vocabulary split. Neither is codebase-wide, making this a spot issue rather than a systemic problem.

abstraction_fitness.dimension_character: The codebase is lean on abstraction layers — no delegation-heavy classes, no facade modules, no one-implementation interfaces — which is healthy for its size. Two specific problems undermine this baseline: the validate layer systematically erases types at the boundary where type safety matters most (all validators accept `Record<string, unknown>`), and LoadMoreButton re-implements the server-side grouping algorithm in full rather than consuming a shared type and result shape. The identity function `foodQuery` is a third but minor ceremony item.
abstraction_fitness.score_rationale: The structural abstraction fitness is genuinely strong (no wrapper chains, no delegation indirection, no interface zoo), which pushes toward 80+. The validate layer's type erasure is a systemic pattern touching 8 functions and 9 import sites — every call site must `as unknown as Record<string, unknown>`, making the typed interfaces decorative rather than enforced. The client-side algorithm duplication creates a real maintenance hazard (the normalize/merge logic in LoadMoreButton already diverges slightly from lib/search.ts). These two issues together cap the score at 74.

dependency_health.dimension_character: The dependency set is exceptionally lean for a Next.js application: three runtime dependencies covering only the framework itself, with all tooling appropriately placed in devDependencies. Every external integration (Reddit API, Google Places, OpenRouter, Nominatim) is accessed via native fetch rather than a client library, avoiding the common pattern of pulling in SDKs for light use. The one genuine concern is @prisma/client sitting in devDependencies — unconventional for a runtime ORM client, but potentially managed by the postinstall build step.
dependency_health.score_rationale: This is a near-exemplary dependency profile for its scale — no duplicate libraries, no heavy deps for light use, no unused packages, no version conflicts visible. The @prisma/client placement in devDependencies is the only real concern: it is a known deployment footgun for Prisma apps (Vercel, Railway, etc. expect it in `dependencies`), and while the postinstall script may compensate, the placement is unconventional and could silently break in prune-devdeps deployment workflows. This single issue prevents a score above 90.

low_level_elegance.dimension_character: The codebase shows uneven craft across its modules. The lib/ utilities (state-machine.ts, geo.ts, rules.ts, fingerprint.ts) are concise and intention-revealing; state-machine.ts in particular is a model of proportionate extraction. The problematic area is the monolithic complexity in lib/validate.ts (10 repetitive guard pairs not matching the table-driven pattern used for simpler cases), the scrape route handler (mixed orchestration + data access + validation in one 180-LOC body), and lib/hours.ts (format-branching parser with non-obvious control flow). These are not individually catastrophic but are consistently denser than the surrounding code.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: About half the codebase functions are clean and appropriately sized, placing a floor around 75. The scrape handler's density (multiple distinct responsibilities, 180 LOC, no extraction) and the validate.ts inconsistency (loop-based for simple cases, manual pairs for complex ones) are recurring friction points that a developer encounters when modifying core flows. lib/hours.ts's complex parser is justified by domain complexity but still requires careful tracing. The overall picture is solid but uneven, fitting the 70-75 band of the global anchors ('a developer would encounter local complexity that makes individual functions harder to follow than they should be').

mid_level_elegance.dimension_character: The vote and search read flows have well-composed seams — each collaborator has a clear input/output contract, and the route handlers act as thin translation layers. The two boundary weaknesses are structural: the search-more/LoadMoreButton seam forces business logic (restaurant grouping) onto the client because the API returns raw results rather than grouped ones, and the scrape trigger in the search handler fires blindly without knowing if a scrape is already running or has recently failed for the same query, creating potential thundering-herd behavior on popular low-result queries.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: The vote flows and basic search path read cleanly as composition — these would score in the 85-90 range alone. The LoadMoreButton grouping duplication is a boundary design failure: the server computed groups for the initial page, but the client must recompute them for additional pages, meaning the boundary was drawn at the wrong level. The uncapped scrape trigger is a workflow integrity issue — repeated queries with few results will spawn unbounded parallel scrapes. Together these two structural seam problems fit the 70 band: a developer making cross-module changes in the load-more or scrape-trigger flows will encounter implicit contracts and surprising re-implementations.

test_strategy.dimension_character: The test suite is strong where it exists: the field-validation, state-machine, and API route tests are well-designed, well-isolated, and cover the intended behaviors systematically. The strategic gap is broad coverage of the lib/ layer and the entire components/ layer. lib/hours.ts is the most acute risk — a 127-LOC parser with complex branching logic, 4 importers, and zero tests. lib/reddit.ts and lib/openrouter.ts represent another 587 LOC of network-interaction and text-processing logic with no tests. The component layer is entirely untested, which is less critical for business logic but means visual regressions and interaction bugs in SearchPoller, LoadMoreButton, and RestaurantFeedbackButton go undetected.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The existing test infrastructure is well-designed and not fragile — this is a genuine positive that sets a floor well above 40. However, the 62 score reflects that a developer making changes to lib/hours.ts, lib/reddit.ts, lib/openrouter.ts, or any component has no test safety net. The tested surfaces (validators, state machine, vote/search routes) are the right surfaces to test, but they cover roughly 30% of production LOC. The global anchor for 60 ('meaningful risk goes unvalidated') applies: changes to the complex parser, the Reddit scraping logic, or the hours-display behavior carry real regression risk with no tests to catch it.

api_surface_coherence.dimension_character: The codebase has a moderate coherence problem across two fronts: parameter naming is inconsistent for the same concept across sibling API routes (q vs query vs terms for the user's search input), and lib modules mix sync and async functions in their public surfaces without separation. The dual-name export on geo.ts (detect_city / detectCityFromIp) additionally creates confusion about which name callers should use, which is confirmed by actual split usage across sibling routes.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The parameter naming inconsistency across API routes is a real developer friction point — a developer integrating a new route would not know whether to call their param `q`, `query`, or `terms`, and would have to look at existing routes individually. The sync/async mixing in lib modules is a lesser issue (each function is individually clear), but together these patterns put this at the 68 range: solid in many places but with recurring API surface irregularities a developer would hit.

authorization_consistency.dimension_character: The codebase has no authentication layer at all — every API route is fully open, including state-mutating endpoints that write votes to the database and trigger expensive third-party API calls. The vote routes use a fingerprint-based deduplication heuristic (IP+UA hash) as a spam guard, but this is explicitly not authentication and provides no identity verification or access control. The absence of auth is total and uniform, which makes it internally consistent but architecturally absent.
authorization_consistency.score_rationale: A score of 55 reflects that the auth posture is not inconsistently applied — it is uniformly absent. For read-only public endpoints (geo, trending, search) the absence may be intentional, but for write endpoints (vote, restaurant-vote) and the expensive scrape endpoint, the lack of any auth boundary is a real security gap. The dimension anchor for 60 is 'auth posture is inconsistent; a developer reviewing security cannot trust that coverage is complete' — here coverage is uniformly zero for mutations, which is arguably worse than inconsistency. The fingerprint guard adds minimal friction but does not constitute authorization.

ai_generated_debt.dimension_character: The codebase shows two distinct debt patterns: scattered debug logging left in production paths (scrape, google-places, openrouter modules each have console.log/warn calls that read as development traces), and copy-pasted algorithmic logic between lib/search.ts and components/LoadMoreButton.tsx. A standalone pass-through no-op function (foodQuery) is a clear AI-scaffolding artifact. The validate.ts file, while systematic, applies redundant double-validation where callers have already done manual checks. The issues are real but not pervasive — most of the codebase is purposeful.
ai_generated_debt.score_rationale: At 76, the codebase is generally purposeful but a developer would wade through debug logging in the scrape flow and encounter the notable duplication of restaurant-grouping logic. The foodQuery no-op is a clear artifact that should be excised. These are enough recurring patterns to be noticeable drag without being pervasive — placing this between the 70 (mixed) and 80 (solid but uneven) anchors.

incomplete_migration.dimension_character: This codebase has essentially no migration debt — no JS/TS mixing, no deprecated symbols, no stale TODOs referencing legacy patterns, no coexisting old/new library choices. The single exception is the geo.ts alias shim (detectCityFromIp = detect_city) where a backward-compatibility alias was added but the original snake_case export was never removed, leaving both names active and in use across sibling routes. The project is young and clean.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: At 80, the dimension is solid but has one genuine incomplete migration artifact: the geo.ts dual-export that has both the original (detect_city) and its alias (detectCityFromIp) live in production. A score above 85 would require zero stale shims or dual-pattern coexistence. The single instance is isolated and low-risk but is a real incomplete migration per the rubric definition.

package_organization.dimension_character: The codebase has a clear and correct three-layer structure (pages, components, library) with no straggler root files, no directory cycles, and correct import direction throughout. lib/ is the one area where structure could improve: it is a flat collection of 11 files spanning external API clients, data access, business logic, and utilities without subdirectory grouping. At this project size the flat structure is defensible, but a developer looking for a specific concern (e.g. external integrations) must scan the entire lib/ listing.
package_organization.score_rationale: At 82, the codebase is solid — the layering is correct, naming is clear, and the Next.js app directory structure is idiomatic. What prevents a higher score is the undifferentiated lib/ directory that mixes fundamentally different concerns: a developer cannot navigate to 'external integrations' or 'business rules' without already knowing the file names. This is a moderate friction point at 11 files but would become a real problem as the project grows.

initialization_coupling.dimension_character: The codebase is largely clean of import-time side effects. The main tension is in lib/reddit.ts, where three environment variables (REDDIT_CLIENT_ID, REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET, REDDIT_USER_AGENT) are captured into module-level constants at import time, creating a snapshot that is frozen for the lifetime of the module. lib/db.ts uses the idiomatic Next.js globalThis PrismaClient pattern and reads NODE_ENV at instantiation, which is an accepted tradeoff for HMR environments. All other lib modules correctly defer env reads into function bodies.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: The codebase scores above average on this dimension. Most env reads happen inside function bodies (google-places, geo, openrouter), which is the correct pattern. The reddit.ts import-time snapshot is a genuine fragility in test contexts and a minor correctness risk if env vars are populated late; the db.ts pattern is idiomatic but carries the same fragility. No network calls, file I/O, or DB connections are made at module scope (PrismaClient construction is deferred to first-use via the lazy singleton). Score of 74 reflects the two real issues (reddit.ts env snapshot, reddit.ts mutable cachedToken global) against an otherwise clean baseline.

design_coherence.dimension_character: The lib layer is mostly well-structured with focused functions, but the API route handlers and components show a recurring pattern of mixing too many responsibilities in single functions. The scrape route is the clearest violation: it is a 180-line orchestration function with no sub-function extraction. LoadMoreButton duplicates the grouping algorithm from lib/search.ts rather than calling it server-side, creating a design split where the same logic runs in two different environments. The VoteButton/RestaurantFeedbackButton duplication is the most actionable design defect — nearly identical components differing only by API endpoint and storage key.
design_coherence.score_rationale: Score of 62 reflects that roughly half the codebase follows good design practices (lib functions are focused, state-machine and validate modules are clean) while the high-signal files (scrape route, LoadMoreButton, TrendingSection useEffect) have genuine multi-responsibility problems that would confuse a new developer. The duplicated grouping algorithm between LoadMoreButton and lib/search.ts is a significant design coherence failure — the same structural logic lives in two places with different implementations, which is exactly the kind of pattern the dimension targets.

contract_coherence.dimension_character: Most functions honor their declared return type contracts — the lib layer is consistent about returning empty arrays on failure rather than null, and validators throw rather than silently returning. The main contract violations are in naming and documentation: the `foodQuery` no-op function claims to do something it doesn't, the `detect_city`/`detectCityFromIp` alias pair documents backward compatibility backwards, and the openrouter.ts JSDoc refers to 'Gemini Flash' while the actual model is Llama. These are correctness documentation failures rather than runtime failures.
contract_coherence.score_rationale: Score of 72 reflects a codebase where runtime contracts are largely honored but documentation contracts have accumulated drift. The `foodQuery` no-op is the only outright behavioral contract lie (a function that claims to be a transformer but is an identity function). The naming inversions and stale model references are lower severity. No functions were found that silently swallow errors while claiming to throw, or that mutate state while named as getters.

logic_clarity.dimension_character: The codebase logic is generally clear and direct. Guard clauses are used consistently, early returns are preferred over deep nesting, and boolean expressions are simplified where possible. The main logic clarity issues are a handful of redundant null-coalescing operators on typed non-nullable values (RestaurantFeedbackButton's `initialUpvotes ?? 0`, the restaurant page's `upvotes ?? 0`), and the async-without-await smells flagged by the detector appear to be false positives upon manual inspection. The `foodQuery` no-op function is the clearest logic clarity violation.
logic_clarity.score_rationale: Score of 70 reflects solid overall logic structure with a few scattered redundant defensive checks. The redundant `?? 0` on typed non-nullable props and the identity function masquerading as a transformer are real issues but are local in scope. No identical branches, dead code after unconditional returns, or always-true conditions were found in the codebase. The async-without-await detector signals appear to be false positives for this TypeScript codebase.

type_safety.dimension_character: The codebase has a structural type safety gap: well-typed interfaces exist throughout (ParsedQuery, RestaurantWithRecommendations, PlaceDetails, RedditPost) but the validation layer accepts Record<string, unknown>, forcing every call site to double-cast through unknown. Additionally, the RestaurantStatus Prisma enum is bypassed with raw string literals in all three component status badge lookups (RestaurantCard, TrendingSection, restaurant page), meaning the type system cannot catch typos or missing cases. Fetch response casting without runtime validation in LoadMoreButton and TrendingSection creates silent failure modes if API shapes drift.
type_safety.score_rationale: Score of 65 reflects a codebase that uses TypeScript intentionally and has good type coverage in the lib layer, but has two systemic weaknesses: the validate.ts type-erasure pattern (8+ double-casts in production code) and the enum bypass pattern in component status lookups. These are not trivial style issues — they are gaps where the type system cannot catch real classes of bugs. The fetch response casts without runtime guards in components add additional exposure at API boundaries.

issues.suggestion: Complete the rename: make detectCityFromIp the canonical export (or rename back to detect_city if snake_case was intentional per spec). Update app/api/trending/route.ts and app/api/search/route.ts to import the camelCase name. Remove the alias export. This is a 3-file change: update the two import sites and remove the alias line from geo.ts.
evidence: lib/geo.ts line 6: exports `detect_city` as the primary function (snake_case)
lib/geo.ts line 40: exports `detectCityFromIp = detect_city` with comment 'Alias for backward compatibility'
app/api/trending/route.ts line 3: `import { detect_city, extractIp } from '@/lib/geo'` — uses the original name
app/api/search/route.ts line 4: `import { detect_city, extractIp } from '@/lib/geo'` — uses the original name
app/api/geo/route.ts line 2: `import { detectCityFromIp, extractIp } from '@/lib/geo'` — uses the alias

issues.summary: lib/ bundles 4 distinct concern domains in a flat directory with no subdirectory grouping
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg: (1) Create lib/integrations/ and move geo.ts, reddit.ts, openrouter.ts, google-places.ts into it; (2) Create lib/domain/ and move rules.ts, validate.ts, state-machine.ts, search.ts into it; (3) Leave db.ts, fingerprint.ts, hours.ts in lib/ as they are genuinely shared utilities. Update all @/lib/* imports to @/lib/integrations/* or @/lib/domain/* as appropriate. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to validate. This is 4 file moves for integrations and 4 for domain, with import updates across app/api/* and components/.
evidence: lib/ has 11 files with avg_fan_in 4.4 — a well-used shared directory, but flat
External API clients: geo.ts (IP API), reddit.ts (Reddit API), openrouter.ts (LLM API), google-places.ts (Google Places API) — four integration files mixed with business logic
Data access: db.ts (Prisma client singleton)
Business logic: rules.ts (voting rules), validate.ts (validators), state-machine.ts (scrape state), search.ts (search orchestration)
Utilities: fingerprint.ts (hashing), hours.ts (hours parsing)
A developer looking for 'where do we call Google Places?' must scan 11 files; with subdirectories they would go to lib/integrations/

issues.summary: Reddit env vars captured as module-level constants — fragile in test contexts
issues.suggestion: Move the env reads inside `getAccessToken()` and `buildHeaders()` — read `process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_ID` and `process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET` lazily at call time. USER_AGENT can remain a constant if the default fallback is acceptable, but for testability it should also be read lazily or passed as a parameter.
evidence: lib/reddit.ts lines 54-56: `const USER_AGENT = process.env.REDDIT_USER_AGENT || 'LocalRecos/1.0'`, `const CLIENT_ID = process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_ID`, `const CLIENT_SECRET = process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET` — all read once at import time.
Any test that imports lib/reddit.ts before setting these env vars will capture undefined/default values for CLIENT_ID and CLIENT_SECRET, permanently disabling OAuth token fetching for that test run.

issues.summary: scrape/route.ts POST handler is a 180-line function with 5 distinct responsibility clusters
issues.suggestion: Extract `enrichAndUpsertResults(enriched, city)` to handle the upsert+community-picks loop, and `runFetchAndFallback(city, query, terms)` to handle the Reddit→LLM fallback logic. The POST handler should read as pure orchestration of named steps.
evidence: Single POST function mixes: HTTP body parsing, state-machine orchestration, Reddit scraping coordination, Google Places enrichment loop, restaurant upsert loop, community-pick insertion loop.
The enrichment and upsert sections (lines 130-210) are each 30-40 lines of sequential DB operations that could be extracted to named helper functions.
Monster function signal confirmed: smells detector flagged 1x Monster function (>150 LOC) and 2x High cyclomatic complexity (>15 branches).

issues.summary: LoadMoreButton re-implements groupByName/shouldMerge/normalizeWebsite already in lib/search.ts
issues.suggestion: Move the grouping logic to a shared utility (e.g. lib/group.ts) that can be imported by both the server-side search.ts and the client component. Alternatively, make the /api/search-more endpoint return pre-grouped results, eliminating the need for client-side grouping entirely.
evidence: LoadMoreButton.tsx lines 57-145 contain normalizeWebsite, shouldMerge, and groupByName — all functionally identical to lib/search.ts normalizeWebsite, shouldMergeGroups, groupRestaurantsByName.

The client-side implementations differ subtly: normalizeWebsite in LoadMoreButton strips trailing slash but doesn't strip path; lib/search.ts strips only the hostname. These divergences will produce different grouping results.
Confirmed by boilerplate_duplication signal and structural signal (large file: mixed jsx_rendering, data_fetching, data_transforms).

issues.summary: VoteButton and RestaurantFeedbackButton share identical cast() logic duplicated verbatim
issues.suggestion: Extract a useOptimisticVote(apiEndpoint, storageKeyPrefix, initialUpvotes, initialDownvotes) hook that owns the cast logic. Both components become thin wrappers that differ only in their rendered UI (RestaurantFeedbackButton shows 'Helpful?' label; VoteButton shows numeric counts).
evidence: Both components implement the same optimistic-update pattern: increment state → fetch → reconcile from server response or revert on error.
The cast() function body is >20 lines and differs only in the API endpoint string (/api/vote vs /api/restaurant-vote) and storage key prefix.

boilerplate_duplication signal: 'Boilerplate block repeated across 2 files (window 10 lines): components/RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx:37, components/VoteButton.tsx:38' (fingerprint: 616191946bfa168e).

issues.summary: TrendingSection useEffect mixes geolocation, IP fallback, and localStorage in one 30-line callback
issues.suggestion: Extract a `useCityDetection()` hook that returns the resolved city string. TrendingSection useEffect then has one responsibility: fetch trending data for the resolved city.
evidence: The useEffect callback contains: geolocation.getCurrentPosition branch, IP-fallback fetch branch, localStorage read, and data-fetching — all interleaved.
High cyclomatic complexity signal confirmed (>15 branches).
fetchIpFallback is a nested function inside the effect — it could be extracted to the component scope for clarity.

issues.summary: Restaurant detail page fetches all city restaurants to compute grouping for a single restaurant
issues.suggestion: Add a `findRestaurantGroup(restaurantId, city)` function in lib/search.ts that queries only restaurants with matching name prefix or shared website, or store group membership explicitly. The page component should call a focused API rather than loading all city data.
evidence: Lines 54-63: `prisma.restaurant.findMany({ where: { city: ... } })` fetches the entire city dataset, then runs groupRestaurantsByName on all of it, just to find the group that contains `params.id`.
For a city with 500 restaurants this is an unbounded full-table scan on every page load.
The design mixes a data-access concern (find my group) with a rendering concern in the page component.

issues.summary: foodQuery() is a named transformation function that returns its input unchanged
issues.suggestion: Remove foodQuery entirely and call `encodeURIComponent(query)` directly, or implement the food-domain transformation the name implies (e.g., appending 'restaurant' or 'food' to help Reddit's search). The name-behavior mismatch should be resolved either way.
evidence: lib/reddit.ts lines 100-102: `function foodQuery(query: string): string { return query; }`

Called at line 108: `const encodedQuery = encodeURIComponent(foodQuery(query))`. The function adds no transformation — it is a pure identity wrapper with a name that promises food-specific query transformation.
Callers that read the call site assume the query has been transformed for food-domain searching. This assumption is false.

issues.summary: openrouter.ts JSDoc says 'Gemini Flash' but code uses Llama model
issues.suggestion: Update the JSDoc for parseQueryWithLLM (and parseQuery in search.ts) to reference the actual model or to describe the behavior generically ('using an LLM via OpenRouter') so the comment does not mislead readers about which model is in use.
evidence: lib/openrouter.ts line 6-8: JSDoc says 'Parse a natural-language restaurant query into city + search terms using Gemini Flash.'
lib/openrouter.ts line 2: `const MODEL = 'meta-llama/llama-3.2-3b-instruct:free'` — not a Gemini model.
lib/search.ts line 13: same Gemini Flash reference in the docstring for parseQuery, which delegates to this function.

issues.summary: detect_city exported as primary name but alias detectCityFromIp documented as backward-compat alias — roles are inverted
issues.suggestion: Rename the primary function to `detectCityFromIp` (camelCase, consistent with TS conventions), remove the alias, and update the three import sites. The snake_case form was introduced to match a spec/manifest name but violates the live codebase convention.
evidence: lib/geo.ts line 6: `export async function detect_city(ip: string)` — uses snake_case, comment says 'Alias: detect_city (used by GeoDetector.detect_city in the spec)'.
lib/geo.ts line 40: `export const detectCityFromIp = detect_city` — comment says 'Alias for backward compatibility'.

app/api/geo/route.ts imports `detectCityFromIp`; app/api/trending/route.ts and app/api/search/route.ts import `detect_city`. The codebase uses both names actively, contradicting the 'backward compatibility' framing of detectCityFromIp.
snake_case name (detect_city) is a convention outlier in a camelCase TypeScript codebase.

issues.summary: initialUpvotes and initialDownvotes typed as number but guarded with ?? 0 in useState
issues.suggestion: Remove the `?? 0` fallbacks: `useState(initialUpvotes)` and `useState(initialDownvotes)`. If the upstream callers might pass undefined, update the prop type to `number | undefined` to make the optionality explicit.
evidence: RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx line 21: `const [upvotes, setUpvotes] = useState(initialUpvotes ?? 0)` — initialUpvotes is typed as `number` in the interface (line 7), so `?? 0` can never trigger.
RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx line 22: same pattern for `initialDownvotes ?? 0`.
The `?? 0` suggests the author was uncertain whether the prop could be undefined, which conflicts with the non-optional type annotation.

issues.summary: foodQuery() returns its input unchanged — dead transformation logic
issues.suggestion: Remove the foodQuery wrapper function and call `encodeURIComponent(query)` directly at line 108. If food-domain enrichment is desired in the future, add it with an explicit implementation at that time.
evidence: lib/reddit.ts lines 100-102: `function foodQuery(query: string): string { return query; }`

A developer reading `encodeURIComponent(foodQuery(query))` will assume some food-domain transformation has occurred. None has. This is a logic clarity violation — the code claims to transform but is an identity.

issues.summary: restaurant.upvotes and restaurant.downvotes guarded with ?? 0 after notFound() null check
issues.suggestion: Simplify to `const totalNetVotes = restaurant.upvotes - restaurant.downvotes`. The null guard adds cognitive load by implying the values might be null when they cannot be.
evidence: Line 51: `if (!restaurant) notFound()` — after this, restaurant is guaranteed non-null.

Line 75: `const totalNetVotes = (restaurant.upvotes ?? 0) - (restaurant.downvotes ?? 0)` — Prisma schema defines upvotes and downvotes as non-nullable Int fields, so these values are guaranteed to be numbers. The `?? 0` is redundant.

issues.summary: All validators accept Record<string, unknown>, forcing double-cast through unknown at every call site
issues.suggestion: Update validator signatures to accept their corresponding typed interfaces: `validateParsedQuery(obj: ParsedQuery): void`, `validateRedditPost(obj: RedditPost): void`, etc. The internal field access can remain as `obj.field` since the interface guarantees the shape. This eliminates all double-casts and allows the type system to catch mismatches at validation call sites.
evidence: lib/validate.ts: validateParsedQuery(obj: Record<string, unknown>), validateRedditPost(obj: Record<string, unknown>), validatePlaceDetails(obj: Record<string, unknown>) — all validators erase the caller's type context.
app/api/scrape/route.ts line 173: `validateRestaurantInput({...} as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)` — typed object cast away for validation.
lib/reddit.ts line 141: `validateRedditPost(redditPost as unknown as Record<string, unknown>)` — same pattern.
The ParsedQuery, RedditPost, PlaceDetails TypeScript interfaces exist in the codebase but are not used as the validator parameter types.

issues.summary: RestaurantStatus Prisma enum bypassed with raw string literals in component status lookups
issues.suggestion: Import RestaurantStatus from '@prisma/client' in the component interfaces and Record types. Change `status: string` to `status: RestaurantStatus` and `Record<string, {...}>` to `Record<RestaurantStatus, {...}>`. The type system will then flag exhaustiveness gaps when the enum changes.
evidence: RestaurantCard.tsx line 40: `status: string` in the Restaurant interface instead of `RestaurantStatus`.

RestaurantCard.tsx line 51: `const STATUS_LABELS: Record<string, {...}>` — string keys 'VERIFIED', 'UNREVIEWED', 'INCOMPLETE'. The type system cannot catch if a new status is added to the Prisma enum.
TrendingSection.tsx line 6: `status: string` in TrendingRestaurant interface.
app/restaurant/[id]/page.tsx line 13: `const STATUS_INFO: Record<string, {...}>` with string keys.
If the Prisma enum adds or renames a status value, TypeScript will not flag the outdated component lookups.

issues.summary: LoadMoreButton casts fetch response to RawRestaurant[] without runtime shape validation
issues.suggestion: Add a type guard or Zod schema for the search-more API response. At minimum, check `Array.isArray(data.results)` before casting. Alternatively, define a shared response type for /api/search-more that is used in both the route handler and the client component.
evidence: LoadMoreButton.tsx line 172: `const newGroups = groupByName(data.results as RawRestaurant[], seenNames)` — `data` is `any` from `res.json()`, cast directly to `RawRestaurant[]`.
If /api/search-more returns an error object or changes its shape, the cast silently succeeds and the subsequent code receives malformed data.
TrendingSection.tsx has the same pattern: `setResults(d.results ?? [])` where `d` is untyped from fetch().json().

add.header: Clean three-layer dependency direction
add.description: lib/ is the domain core; app/api/ and app/pages consume it; components/ consume only lib/hours and lib/geo. No upward imports exist. The direction is consistently correct and enforced by structure.

add.header: No import cycles in the graph
add.description: The import graph contains no circular dependencies. lib/reddit.ts has a mid-file import statement (line 52) but this does not create a cycle — it is a style outlier only.

add.header: Grouping algorithm split across server and client
add.description: The restaurant merge/group logic exists in lib/search.ts for the server path and is re-implemented in components/LoadMoreButton.tsx for the client pagination path. The two normalizeWebsite implementations already diverge.

add.header: Domain-aligned lib/ layer with named rules
add.description: lib/ maps cleanly to domain capabilities: db, geo, search, reddit, google-places, hours, rules, validate, state-machine, fingerprint. lib/rules.ts directly references RULE_01/03/05 from the spec, making traceability immediate.

add.header: Predictable API route-to-lib mapping
add.description: Each app/api/ route imports from lib/ only; the handler-to-domain mapping is consistent and a new engineer can trace any API call to its lib module without ambiguity.

add.header: Scrape route doubles as orchestrator
add.description: app/api/scrape/route.ts contains the full pipeline coordination and a local upsertRestaurant helper that belong in lib/. This is the one route where HTTP and domain logic are not separated.

add.header: camelCase dominant in lib/ with one snake_case outlier
add.description: lib/ uses camelCase for all 48 named exports except detect_city which was named to match a spec alias. The outlier is not a style island — it is a single function in an otherwise consistent module.

add.header: Consistent default export pattern in components/
add.description: All 11 component files use default export — no named-vs-default mixing in the component layer.

add.header: Optimistic vote pattern duplicated in dead and live sibling
add.description: VoteButton.tsx (0 importers) and RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx implement the same optimistic update/revert protocol with different API endpoints. The dead file creates convention ambiguity.

add.header: HTTP layer has consistent try/catch + JSON error pattern
add.description: All app/api/ route handlers use try/catch and return NextResponse.json with error fields and appropriate status codes. This layer is consistent even though the lib/ layer it calls is not.

add.header: Vote component siblings have consistent error recovery
add.description: RestaurantFeedbackButton.tsx and VoteButton.tsx both implement optimistic update with catch-based revert. The pattern is consistent within this sibling pair.

add.header: lib/ has no unified error contract across modules
add.description: validate.ts throws, geo.ts returns null, openrouter.ts returns fallback values, reddit.ts silently skips individual failures. Callers must read each implementation to predict failure behavior.

add.header: Precise domain-aligned prefix taxonomy in lib/
add.description: lib/ uses validate, is, parse, get, build, fetch, check, assert prefixes consistently. No generic process/handle/do/run verbs appear in domain code. A developer can predict behavior from names in nearly all cases.

add.header: Rule function names link directly to spec
add.description: checkRule01, checkRule03, checkRule05, tryTransitionToVerified use the RULE_ catalog numbering, giving new engineers immediate traceability between code and requirements.

add.header: foodQuery no-op placeholder in lib/reddit.ts
add.description: foodQuery() is named as a transformation function but returns its input unchanged. The name misleads readers about whether query enrichment is happening in the Reddit search path.

add.header: Zero wrapper/delegation layers in lib/
add.description: No delegation-heavy classes, facade modules, or single-implementation interface ecosystems exist. Every lib/ export is a direct function or class with genuine behavior, keeping the abstraction stack flat and navigable.

add.header: Validate layer erases types at enforcement boundary
add.description: All 8 validator functions in lib/validate.ts accept `Record<string, unknown>` instead of the typed interfaces they guard. Callers must double-cast with `as unknown as Record<string, unknown>`, making the TypeScript interfaces decorative rather than enforced at the point of validation.

add.header: Client-side grouping algorithm duplicated from lib/search.ts
add.description: LoadMoreButton reimplements the full restaurant grouping logic (normalizeWebsite, shouldMerge, groupByName) that already exists in lib/search.ts, with parallel but diverging type definitions. The two implementations have already diverged in the normalizeWebsite function.

add.header: Minimal production dependency surface
add.description: Only next, react, and react-dom are production dependencies. All external API integrations use native fetch, avoiding SDK-for-light-use bloat entirely.

add.header: Native fetch used uniformly for all external calls
add.description: No HTTP client library (axios, got, ky) is present. All five external services (Reddit, Google Places, OpenRouter, ip-api, Nominatim) are called via native fetch, maintaining a single consistent HTTP boundary.

add.header: @prisma/client misplaced in devDependencies
add.description: @prisma/client is in devDependencies despite being a runtime dependency (imported by lib/db.ts, the most-imported file in the codebase). The postinstall `prisma generate` step may compensate in some environments but this is non-standard and deployment-fragile.

add.header: lib/state-machine.ts is a model of proportionate extraction
add.description: All three exported functions are 2-4 lines, the VALID_TRANSITIONS table is the single source of truth, and the error class is precise. This file demonstrates the extraction level the rest of the codebase aspires to.

add.header: Scrape handler mixes orchestration and data access
add.description: The 180-LOC POST function in app/api/scrape/route.ts interleaves state machine transitions, external API calls, and nested DB upsert loops without extraction. This is the primary low-level density hotspot in the codebase.

add.header: validate.ts uses two inconsistent guard styles
add.description: validateRedditPost uses a loop over a string-fields array, while all other validators repeat null/empty pairs manually. The inconsistency doubles code volume and makes field additions error-prone.

add.header: Vote and search read flows are clean seam compositions
add.description: The POST /api/vote and POST /api/restaurant-vote handlers compose fingerprinting, rule checking, transaction, and state transition as clearly named collaborators. The GET /api/search handler composes parseQuery, searchRestaurants, and groupRestaurantsByName in a linear pipeline. These seams are explicit, minimal, and unsurprising.

add.header: Search-more boundary forces grouping logic onto client
add.description: The /api/search endpoint returns grouped results but /api/search-more returns raw rows, pushing the grouping algorithm onto LoadMoreButton. This boundary asymmetry is the root cause of the client-side algorithm duplication.

add.header: Blind scrape trigger on every low-result search
add.description: The search handler triggers a scrape unconditionally when grouped results are fewer than 3, with no deduplication, cooldown, or in-flight awareness. This is a potential source of redundant external API calls under normal traffic.

add.header: API route tests are well-designed and well-isolated
add.description: All 5 API route test files use vi.mock() to isolate the route handler from lib collaborators, test the full HTTP contract (status codes, response shapes, error conditions), and cover the key branching paths. No fragility patterns observed.

add.header: State-machine and field-validation tests are exhaustive
add.description: state-machine.test.ts covers every valid and invalid transition pair (T68-T132). field-validation.test.ts covers all required-field constraints for all 7 entity types (T01-T57). These are the kind of adversarial tests that catch contract changes immediately.

add.header: No snapshot tests — snapshot overuse is not a concern
add.description: The test suite uses behavioral assertions throughout. No snapshot tests are present, so test fragility from snapshot churn is not a risk.

add.header: Large lib/ surface untested: hours, reddit, openrouter
add.description: lib/hours.ts (127 LOC), lib/reddit.ts (436 LOC), and lib/openrouter.ts regexFallback are untested pure-function surfaces. Together they represent ~600 LOC of domain logic with no regression safety net.

add.header: lib/rules.ts tested only through mocked boundaries
add.description: The DB query logic in checkRule01/03/05 and the multi-step tryTransitionToVerified are never directly exercised by any test — they are always replaced by vi.fn() mocks in API tests.

add.header: Validate.ts public API is shape-coherent
add.description: All eight public validate functions follow an identical (obj: Record<string, unknown>) => void signature and throw ValidationError, making the validation module's API surface internally consistent even if the untyped parameter is a compromise.

add.header: Vote route pair is structurally consistent
add.description: app/api/vote/route.ts and app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts follow an identical POST body pattern, validation sequence, and response shape — the two parallel vote endpoints are coherent siblings.

add.header: Search param naming drift across API routes
add.description: The user's raw search input is called 'q' in /api/search, 'query' in /api/scrape, and is absent (pre-split into 'terms') in /api/search-more — a developer adding a new route cannot infer the right parameter name from prior examples.

add.header: Snake case / camelCase dual export on geo.ts
add.description: detect_city (snake_case) and detectCityFromIp (camelCase) coexist as two live exports for the same function, with sibling routes each using a different name.

add.header: No authentication layer exists in any route
add.description: All seven API routes have zero auth checks. The product appears to be an anonymous-first experience, but write endpoints and expensive backend triggers are fully open to any caller.

add.header: Fingerprint-based vote deduplication is not auth
add.description: lib/fingerprint.ts and lib/rules.ts implement IP+UA hashing and duplicate-vote checks as integrity guards, not authentication. These reduce spam but do not establish identity or restrict access.

add.header: Scrape endpoint is internally triggered but publicly reachable
add.description: app/api/scrape/route.ts is invoked internally via triggerScrape() in /api/search, but is also reachable as a public HTTP endpoint, enabling any client to trigger full Google Places + Reddit + LLM pipeline at no cost to them.

add.header: No restating comments in lib modules
add.description: The lib/ directory has minimal commenting — comments that exist explain WHY (business rules, spec references, edge cases) not WHAT the code does. The comment-to-code ratio of 0.049 reflects deliberate sparseness rather than bloat.

add.header: foodQuery no-op scaffolding artifact
add.description: lib/reddit.ts contains a zero-logic pass-through function foodQuery() that is called in production but does nothing — a clear AI-generated placeholder that was never implemented or removed.

add.header: Debug trace logs left in scrape production path
add.description: app/api/scrape/route.ts, lib/google-places.ts, and lib/openrouter.ts each contain console.log calls that trace execution flow, adding noise on every production request.

add.header: Restaurant grouping algorithm duplicated across server and client
add.description: The shouldMerge/normalizeWebsite/groupByName logic is duplicated between lib/search.ts (server) and LoadMoreButton.tsx (client) because the server module contains Prisma imports that prevent direct client-side use.

add.header: Codebase is new with no migration debt
add.description: No JS/TS mixing, no deprecated symbols, no legacy library coexistence (axios/fetch, moment/dayjs), and no migration TODOs found. The project is a fresh Next.js 14 + Prisma stack with no meaningful legacy surface.

add.header: Single incomplete rename on geo.ts detect_city
add.description: lib/geo.ts has both detect_city (snake_case original) and detectCityFromIp (camelCase alias) actively exported and used by different sibling routes — the rename was started but not completed.

add.header: Three-layer app/components/lib structure is correct
add.description: The top-level directory layout maps cleanly to pages, UI components, and shared library code with no cycles and correct import direction throughout. The coupling_matrix shows only two edges: app→components and components→lib, both in the right direction.

add.header: Next.js api/ subdirectory-per-route is well-organized
add.description: Each API route lives in its own subdirectory (geo/, vote/, scrape/, etc.) following idiomatic Next.js App Router conventions, making routes independently navigable.

add.header: lib/ directory mixes four distinct concern domains flat
add.description: lib/ contains external API clients (geo, reddit, openrouter, google-places), data access (db), business logic (rules, validate, state-machine, search), and utilities (fingerprint, hours) all in one flat directory with no subdirectory grouping.

add.header: No straggler root-level production files
add.description: All production TypeScript files are under their appropriate directories. Root-level files are config only (next.config.js, tsconfig.json, etc.).

add.header: PrismaClient uses Next.js globalThis singleton pattern
add.description: lib/db.ts follows the documented Next.js pattern for reusing PrismaClient across HMR reloads. It reads NODE_ENV at instantiation time, which is a known and accepted tradeoff — not a defect in the Next.js context.

add.header: Most lib modules defer env reads to function bodies
add.description: lib/geo.ts, lib/google-places.ts, lib/openrouter.ts, lib/fingerprint.ts all read process.env inside function bodies rather than at module scope. This is the correct pattern and makes them safe to import before env vars are populated.

add.header: reddit.ts captures env vars as module-level constants
add.description: CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET, and USER_AGENT are frozen at import time. This is fragile in test contexts and means test authors must set env vars before any import of this module.

add.header: reddit.ts cachedToken is a module-level mutable singleton
add.description: The OAuth token cache is stored at module scope, shared across all request handlers. This is intentional for performance but creates implicit shared state that leaks between tests and requests.

add.header: Next.js API routes flagged orphaned by static import analysis
add.description: All app/api/* route files and app/*/page.tsx files have zero static importers because Next.js discovers them via filesystem routing. The orphaned detector cannot distinguish these entry points from genuinely dead code.

add.header: prisma/seed.ts is a CLI script, not an importable module
add.description: seed.ts is invoked via `npx prisma db seed` and correctly has no importers. The orphaned and test_coverage signals are false positives. The hardcoded URLs are expected seed data.

add.header: Grouping algorithm duplicated between server and client
add.description: groupRestaurantsByName in lib/search.ts and groupByName in components/LoadMoreButton.tsx implement the same merging logic with subtly different normalizeWebsite implementations. This creates a correctness risk where server and client produce different grouping results for the same data.

add.header: Vote component optimistic-update pattern duplicated verbatim
add.description: VoteButton and RestaurantFeedbackButton share identical cast() logic. The duplication is not intentional variation — both implement the same fetch-then-reconcile pattern differing only by endpoint URL and storage key.

add.header: lib layer functions are well-focused
add.description: lib/validate.ts, lib/state-machine.ts, lib/fingerprint.ts, lib/rules.ts, and lib/geo.ts are all focused single-responsibility modules with no mixing of concerns. This is a positive pattern worth preserving.

add.header: Runtime return-type contracts are consistently honored
add.description: All lib functions that declare array return types return arrays on all paths (never null). Validator functions throw or return void — no silent swallowing. The runtime behavior matches declared types throughout the lib layer.

add.header: foodQuery is a documented no-op pending implementation
add.description: The function exists as an identity wrapper with a meaningful name, suggesting food-domain query transformation was planned but not implemented. The name-behavior gap misleads callers.

add.header: detect_city uses snake_case against camelCase codebase convention
add.description: The function was named to match a spec document reference but the camelCase alias detectCityFromIp is the idiomatic name for this codebase. Both are actively used, creating a naming inconsistency at the API boundary.

add.header: Guard clause style used consistently throughout lib layer
add.description: All lib functions use early-return guard clauses rather than deep nesting. isValidRestaurantName, isPostRelevantToQuery, and all validate* functions follow this pattern consistently.

add.header: Async-without-await smells appear to be false positives
add.description: The detector flagged lib/search.ts and app/search/page.tsx but manual inspection found all async functions contain explicit await calls. Next.js server components may trigger the detector because the async keyword is sometimes required by framework conventions even when awaits are present.

add.header: Defensive ?? 0 guards on Prisma non-nullable integer fields
add.description: Several components apply ?? 0 to values that Prisma returns as non-nullable Int fields. This adds noise suggesting optional values where the type system guarantees a number.

add.header: lib layer interfaces are well-typed but don't flow into validators
add.description: ParsedQuery, RedditPost, ExtractedRestaurant, PlaceDetails, and RestaurantWithRecommendations are all well-defined typed interfaces. The type safety gap is specifically at the validation boundary where these types are erased to Record<string, unknown>.

add.header: RestaurantStatus enum bypassed with string literals in components
add.description: All three status display components use string-keyed Records rather than the Prisma RestaurantStatus enum. This is likely because Prisma types are considered server-only, but the enum values are needed on the client. A client-safe type alias or Zod schema would solve this without importing Prisma client-side.

add.header: lib/state-machine.ts uses exhaustive literal union types
add.description: ScrapeState is a literal union type and VALID_TRANSITIONS is typed as Record<ScrapeState, ScrapeState[]>, ensuring all states are covered. This is a positive pattern for type-safe state machines.

add.header: External API response types handled correctly at boundaries
add.description: lib/google-places.ts and lib/openrouter.ts correctly type external API responses as unknown/Record<string, unknown> and narrow explicitly before use. The boundary handling is sound even without generated client types.
